claimText: The Blindsight chip is a Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) implant designed to restore vision by bypassing the retina.
evidenceExcerpt: Blindsight is a BCI implant aiming to restore vision by bypassing the retina.
examUse: Statement-based question on the application and mechanism of emerging BCI technologies.
distractorHooks: Repairs the retina instead of bypassing it

claimText: The Blindsight implant restores vision by directly stimulating the visual cortex using a microelectrode array.
evidenceExcerpt: It directly stimulates the visual cortex... It consists of a microelectrode array embedded in the visual cortex capable of stimulating neurons.
examUse: Statement-based question on how the Blindsight BCI interacts with the human brain.
distractorHooks: Uses optical sensors instead of microelectrodes

claimText: The visual cortex is the outermost layer of nerve cell tissues responsible for processing visual data.
evidenceExcerpt: It directly stimulates the visual cortex, the outermost layer of nerve cell tissues responsible for processing visual data.
examUse: Basic science statement paired with technology questions on BCI.
distractorHooks: Innermost layer of nerve cell tissues
